meta.title: ML230/320/350 Petrol Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Mercedes ML230, ML320 & ML350 petrol engines — M111 and M112. Real UK costs, oil leak & intake manifold insight, and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

@graph.name: Mercedes ML Petrol Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes ML230, ML320, or ML350 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M111 or M112 petrol engine typically costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only. Used engines are available from £1,500–£3,500. A full engine replacement can run anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes engine replacements typically around £3,500 or more. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines are no longer available.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes ML320 or ML350 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M112 V6 is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most durable petrol engines. It's free from the diesel-related EGR/DPF problems and the balance shaft issues of the later M272 engine. However, it has known age-related issues: oil leaks (valve cover gaskets, oil cooler seals), intake manifold problems, and camshaft position sensor failures. With proper maintenance, these engines can exceed 288,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes ML petrol engines?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are oil leaks (valve cover gaskets and oil cooler seals), intake manifold and VIM actuator problems, camshaft position sensor failures, high fuel consumption, and age-related cooling system issues. The M112 V6 is generally reliable but these issues are common on higher-mileage examples.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes ML petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £6,000 ML350, a £4,500 recon is 75% — exceeding our 70% Mercedes threshold. Against a tired £3,000 example, the same recon significantly exceeds the threshold — get a scrap valuation first. Well-maintained MLs are becoming rarer — a proper restoration is often justified for a clean example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes ML petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. ML230 (1998–2005): M111.977. ML320 (1998–2003): M112.942. ML350 (2003–2005): M112.970. The badge on the tailgate is the easiest tell — 'ML 230', 'ML 320', or 'ML 350'.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the oil leaks on the M112, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — oil leaks are well-understood repairs. Valve cover gasket replacement and oil cooler seal replacement are common fixes costing £300–£800. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Left unchecked, oil loss can lead to low oil levels and potential engine damage. These are repairs, not engine replacement issues.

eraMap.title: The ML PETROL ENGINES THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The entry-level petrol ML. Reliable but underpowered for a 2-ton SUV.
The original V6 ML. Smooth, torquey, and one of Mercedes' most durable petrol engines.
The bored-out M112 — more power, same reliability. High fuel consumption is the main drawback.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes ML Petrol Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: No longer available from Mercedes — these engines are out of production

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the M111 and M112 petrol engines. A full engine replacement can run anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes engine replacements typically around £3,500 or more. Reconditioned engines typically cost £1,200–£2,200 supply only. Main dealer new engines are no longer available.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an M-Class petrol engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare ML petrol engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover Gaskets & Oil Cooler Seals (M112 V6)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, low oil level warning, visible oil residue around the valve covers or oil cooler area.
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£7,500 (if left untreated leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are one of the most frequent issues with Mercedes V6 engines. The valve cover gaskets and oil cooler seals degrade over time. The M112 engine is known for minor common faults like oil leaks. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Left unchecked, oil loss can lead to low oil levels and potential engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

problems.title: Intake Manifold & VIM Actuator Issues (M112 V6)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, rough idle, engine warning light, hesitation on acceleration, rattling from the intake manifold.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (intake manifold repair or replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The M112 V6 has a variable-length intake manifold (VIM) with tumble flaps. The VIM actuator can fail, causing poor performance and check engine lights. The M112 may also suffer from intake manifold issues. A repair kit is available for some models. If you experience rough running or power loss, have the intake manifold inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full intake manifold failure guide

problems.title: Camshaft Position Sensor Failure (M112 V6)
problems.symptoms: Check engine light, rough idle, poor acceleration, engine stalling, difficult starting.
problems.repairCost: £100–£300 (camshaft position sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£7,500 (if timing issues lead to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: Camshaft position sensors are a known failure point on the M112 V6. They are relatively inexpensive and straightforward to replace. If you experience rough running or a check engine light, have the sensors diagnosed.

failureLink.label: Read the full camshaft sensor failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: ML Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Well-understood repair; cheaper than engine replacement
Repair kit available; catch it early
Uncommon but possible on high-mileage examples
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This ML Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with evidence of regular oil changes
* No oil leaks or evidence of recent gasket replacement
* No check engine lights or rough running
* Post-2003 ML350 preferred (more power, same reliability)
* Well-maintained M112 engines are known to exceed 288,000 miles
avoidIf: * Oil leaks with no documented repair history
* Check engine light with intake manifold or sensor codes
* Rough idle or hesitation with no diagnosis
* Poor service history — these engines are reliable but need good maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The W163 M-Class petrol engines — the M111 four-cylinder and M112 V6 — represent a simpler, more reliable era of Mercedes engineering. The M112 V6 in particular is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most durable petrol engines, with examples exceeding 288,000 miles. It's free from the diesel-related EGR/DPF problems and the balance shaft issues of the later M272. The main drawbacks are high fuel consumption and age-related maintenance: oil leaks, intake manifold issues, and sensor failures. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained ML petrol is a £4,000–£10,000 used SUV; a neglected one is a £5,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare ML petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes ML230, ML320, or ML350 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rough idle, hesitation, or ticking (camshaft sensor or intake manifold warning)
* Service History — including which engine (M111 or M112) and confirmed oil-change intervals
* Oil Leak Check — Check for oil spots under the car or visible residue around valve covers (common on M112)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any intake manifold, camshaft sensor, or emissions codes?
* Power Check — Test acceleration; any hesitation or loss of power? (intake manifold or sensor warning)
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke can indicate valve seal wear; white smoke can indicate coolant issues
* Transmission Check — The 7G-Tronic transmission requires fluid changes every 60,000 km
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: * M111.977 (ML230)

items.label: * M112.970 (ML350)
items.value: Displacement: 3,724 cc · Cylinders: V6, 90° configuration, SOHC, 3 valves per cylinder · Compression Ratio: 10.0:1
